note: Lab-specific proposal content for the site Pitch section. Task examples reference heldout_task_candidates.json by id so SEC citations and rubric checks stay single-sourced.

labs.label: OpenAI proposal
labs.headline: Start with TKO: it solved it, then lost it.
labs.thesis: I would lead OpenAI with TKO because the trace is clean: GPT-5.5 found the filing value, then replaced the requested basis with a plausible spot-price valuation. Evidence was in hand, so the trace motivates a basis-selection hypothesis rather than a retrieval diagnosis. GPT-5.6 Sol, run on the identical twenty within 72 hours of release, made the same TKO substitution with a different wrong number. That is a reason to test the behavior on fresh tasks, not proof of a training defect.

lead_failure.title: TKO valuation-basis precedence
lead_failure.soundbite: Right retrieval, wrong basis, bad final decision.
lead_failure.why_it_matters: This is a candidate reward specification. The model had the answer in the trace, so a pilot would test whether a basis-preservation check improves filing precedence without blocking legitimate recomputation. GPT-5.5 submitted $3.92 billion and GPT-5.6 Sol submitted $4.00 billion after searching the literal phrase 'aggregate value of $3.25 billion'.

backup_failure.title: General Mills guidance vintage
backup_failure.soundbite: It counted the beats, then used the wrong guidance vintage.
backup_failure.why_it_matters: This backs up TKO with a second convention miss: the model must separate original guidance from later revised guidance before scoring beat or miss.

pilot.ask: Run a two-week design and diagnostic to define a sealed finance-agent pilot.
deliverables: A proposed 200-task plan: 120 train, 20 development, and 60 sealed eval tasks.
Reference answers, atomic rubrics, and expert-adjudication notes for the agreed scope.
A labeled baseline sample with tool calls, evidence, output, and cost.
A versioned verifier and reward specification for the selected failure families.
A sealed evaluation plan. The tuned comparison follows when the customer's tuning path is ready.
pilot.success_gate: Proposed before tuning: a paired sealed-task lift with the 95 percent lower bound above zero, improvement across multiple subtypes, no broader regression, and no increase in unsupported citations or premature submissions. The exact threshold is agreed with the lab after the baseline.

product.title: Basis-selection reward pack
product.what_we_build: Tasks where the filing contains an authoritative value and the trace also contains a tempting alternate basis. The verifier checks whether the model follows the requested basis before computing. Matched counterexamples require legitimate recomputation, so the sealed set can detect an overcorrection.

product.title: Completeness and omission-control pack
product.what_we_build: Multi-part finance tasks with strict term coverage. The verifier rewards every requested field and penalizes confident partial answers that omit one deal term, security term, or causal window.
fixes: Security summaries that include exact offering size, restrictions, and redemption.
Merger summaries that include close date, consideration, fractional-share treatment, and delisting.
Causal-window answers that tie facts to the period the question asks about.

labs.label: Anthropic proposal
labs.headline: Start with Delta: facts landed, framing missed.
labs.thesis: I would lead Anthropic with Delta. Fable 5 built a strong guidance breakdown, but did not frame EPS guidance as year-over-year growth. The facts landed. The investor framing missed. Zillow is the backup because it shows the same omission pattern in acquisition strategy.

lead_failure.title: Delta guidance framing
lead_failure.why_it_matters: This is the kind of miss a finance customer feels. The answer sounds competent, but the decision comparison is incomplete because the required investor framing never gets stated.

backup_failure.title: Zillow revenue-trend direction
backup_failure.soundbite: It had the acquisition facts and rationale, then missed the trend direction.
backup_failure.why_it_matters: Zillow is the backup because it shows the same coverage discipline problem without relying on a benchmark-quality story.

pilot.ask: Run a two-week design and diagnostic around Fable 5's highest observed miss families.
deliverables: A proposed 200-task plan: 120 train, 20 development, and 60 sealed eval tasks.
Reference answers with investor-framing checks and omission-sensitive rubrics for the agreed scope.
A labeled baseline sample that marks where facts entered context and where framing was lost.
A verifier package for the selected completeness, retrieval, and basis mechanisms.
A sealed evaluation plan. The tuned comparison follows when the customer's tuning path is ready.
pilot.success_gate: Proposed before tuning: a paired sealed-task lift with the 95 percent lower bound above zero, improvement across coverage and convention subtypes, no retrieval regression, and no increase in unsupported claims. The exact threshold is agreed with the lab after the baseline.

product.title: Investor-framing and completeness pack
product.what_we_build: Tasks where the model can find the facts but must preserve the exact investor comparison: year-over-year growth, trend direction, missing term, or causal window.
fixes: Guidance answers that explicitly state the comparison the investor needs.
Acquisition-strategy answers that tie actions to the requested revenue trend.
Security-term summaries that do not stop after the salient economics.

product.title: Basis-selection eval slice
product.what_we_build: Tasks where multiple conventions are plausible and the model must pick the one requested by the prompt or filing. The rubric scores the decision boundary before the arithmetic.

product.title: Multi-document retrieval coverage pack
product.what_we_build: Tasks that require more than endpoint retrieval. The verifier checks whether every period or filing needed by the question actually entered context before answer generation.
fixes: Multi-year trend retrieval with every period present.
Close-status retrieval that combines announcement and closing filings.
Penalty for answering from a single adjacent source when the prompt requires several.
